title: NPV: A project has cash flows of $20,000, $30,000, and 50000...
summary: Calculate the NPV of a project by discounting expected cash flows at the appropriate discount rate.
prompt: A project has cash flows of $20,000, $30,000, and $50,000 over three years. What is the NPV if the discount rate is 8%?
solution: NPV = -Initial Investment + (Cash Flow / (1 + r)^t). Assuming no initial investment, NPV = ($20,000 / 1.08^1) + ($30,000 / 1.08^2) + ($50,000 / 1.08^3) = $66,000.
